tests.name: init_sh_exits_zero
tests.details: init.sh exited 1 due to interactive prompt for git_host AFTER project skeleton was already fully created. Heredoc ran out of stdin during create_and_protect_remote() (line 1688), causing prompt_choice to loop indefinitely until SIGPIPE-style EOF. All file outputs were created successfully before this failure.

framework_bugs.summary: init.sh has no scriptable mode for UAT — heredoc driver fragile
framework_bugs.reproduction: Pipe canned answers via heredoc to bash init.sh. Project skeleton creates successfully, but create_and_protect_remote() prompts for git_host/visibility/remote_url with no way to script them. With stdin exhausted, prompt_choice() loops 'Invalid choice. Enter a number between 1 and 4.' until SIGPIPE/EOF, then init.sh exits 1.
framework_bugs.expected: init.sh should either accept --non-interactive flags + a config file, or handle EOF on stdin gracefully (default to skip/abort the remote creation step) so the heredoc-driven init returns 0 when project skeleton is fully built.
framework_bugs.actual: init.sh exits 1; project skeleton is intact but no git remote was added, no manifest.json updates for host/mode/remote_url, no branch protection attestation.

documentation_gaps.gap: Runbook tells agents to ground 'platform' as 'mcp_server' but framework canonical name is 'mcp-server' (hyphen). The platform module file is mcp-server.md and the release pipeline marker reads platforms=...,mcp-server. Agents have to translate.
documentation_gaps.where: Reports/uat-2026-04-25/RUNBOOK.md test-cases column 'platform_module_present' and config schema (line 51)

documentation_gaps.gap: Runbook lists hypothetical --non-interactive flags but the actual scriptable surface is missing. Recommend adding --config FILE.json mode that reads project_name, description, platform, track, deployment, language, project_dir, git_host, repo_visibility, remote_url and bypasses all prompts.

init.sh fallback path for 'fake remote' (per runbook constraint 'no real network calls beyond git remote add origin https://example.com/fake.git') is undocumented — runbook says framework knows how to operate against a fake remote but the code does not (host-aware path requires gh/glab CLI; 'other' path tries to push and exits 1 on failure).

tests.name: phase1_to_phase2_gate_passes
tests.details: After stubbing PRODUCT_MANIFESTO.md and PROJECT_BIBLE.md from templates and manually setting current_phase=2 + gate dates in YYYY-MM-DD form, scripts/check-phase-gate.sh proceeds (warns on APPROVAL_LOG.md entries — expected, since APPROVAL_LOG was not populated). Gate logic correctly enforces artifact + approval-log presence.

tests.name: phase_state_advances_to_2
tests.details: Phase advance is manual (no auto-advance script found in framework). Manual update to .claude/phase-state.json is the documented procedure.

documentation_gaps.gap: check-phase-gate.sh requires gate dates in YYYY-MM-DD format (regex anchored), but other framework code (process-checklist.sh:170-180) writes ISO-8601 timestamps for started_at fields. A Phase 0->1 timestamp like '2026-04-25T17:02:03Z' is silently treated as missing.

No documented script to advance current_phase between phases. Agents must hand-edit .claude/phase-state.json. Recommend a 'scripts/process-checklist.sh --advance-phase N' command that runs the gate, refuses on failure, updates current_phase + gate date, and appends to APPROVAL_LOG.md.

tests.name: start_feature_records_state
tests.details: After --start-feature uat-feat-1, .build_loop.feature = 'uat-feat-1'

tests.name: each_step_completes_in_order
tests.details: All 6 build_loop steps completed in sequence with stub artifacts (src/feat1.ts, tests/feat1.test.ts, docs/security-audits/uat-feat-1-security-audit.md). Sequential enforcement verified: step rejection messages for prior-step prereqs.

tests.name: pre_commit_passes_after_loop
tests.details: git commit -m 'feat(uat-1): stub feature for UAT' succeeded after first 5 build_loop steps complete (feature_recorded is step 6 / not required at commit time — intentional per script comments)

tests.name: bl006_blocks_feat_without_loop
tests.details: After clearing .build_loop, simulated PreToolUse hook input '{"command":"git commit --allow-empty -m \"feat(bl006-trigger): no loop\""}' to scripts/pre-commit-gate.sh produced JSON permissionDecision='deny' with 'feat(...) commit blocked — no Build Loop active'. NOTE: scripts/process-checklist.sh --check-commit-message also returns RC=1 in CLI form. The git pre-commit hook itself does NOT call this check (it only runs gitleaks/Semgrep/TDD ordering), so a direct shell git commit is NOT blocked — Claude Code is the only enforcement vector. This is by design.

tests.name: bl015_sentinel_blocks_commit
tests.details: Wrote .claude/pending-approval.json with valid CDF 4.2.3 schema; pre-commit-gate.sh hook returned permissionDecision='deny' with full pending question + options + recommendation surfaced in reason.

tests.name: bl015_sentinel_resolves
tests.details: scripts/pending-approval.sh --resolve removed sentinel; subsequent commit attempt was no longer blocked by sentinel (was blocked by phase2_init verification — separate gate, expected)

tests.name: recorded_feature_appears_in_progress
tests.details: .claude/build-progress.json .features_completed contains 'uat-feat-1'

documentation_gaps.gap: scripts/pending-approval.sh is NOT copied into the project by init.sh (verified: only the framework copy at $FRAMEWORK/scripts/pending-approval.sh exists). Documentation in pre-commit-gate.sh's deny message tells the user to run 'scripts/pending-approval.sh --resolve' but the project has no such script.
documentation_gaps.where: init.sh:1019-1056 (script-copy block) — missing cp for scripts/pending-approval.sh

documentation_gaps.gap: BL-006 enforcement only fires through Claude Code's PreToolUse hook channel; a direct shell `git commit` never invokes it. Builder's Guide / runbook should make explicit that Build Loop enforcement is conditional on Claude Code being the actor, and consider whether to install a real git commit-msg hook to extend the gate to non-Claude commits.

tests.details: All 9 UAT_STEPS (agents_dispatched, template_generated, orchestrator_notified, results_received, completeness_verified, bugs_consolidated, triage_complete, remediation_complete, gate_passed) completed sequentially, RC=0 for each

tests.name: phase3_to_phase4_gate
tests.details: Manual phase advance to 4; no blocking errors from check-phase-gate.sh for the Phase 3->4 transition with stubbed artifacts

documentation_gaps.gap: lint-uat-scenarios.sh usage is non-obvious — runbook says 'run against generated UAT scenario' but the linter takes a single populated HTML file argument and (correctly) flags unreplaced placeholders. Recommend either (a) a flag like '--mode template' to skip placeholder checks against the canonical template, or (b) explicit runbook guidance that the linter only applies after a session HTML is materialized into tests/uat/sessions/.

framework_bugs.summary: phase4_release step order forces HANDOFF.md before handoff_written
framework_bugs.reproduction: Run sequential phase4_release steps. monitoring_configured (step 4) requires HANDOFF.md to exist AND mention monitoring. handoff_written (step 5) is the step that documents creating HANDOFF.md.
framework_bugs.expected: Either reorder PHASE4_STEPS so handoff_written precedes monitoring_configured, OR change the monitoring_configured artifact check to look at a separate monitoring-config doc rather than HANDOFF.md.
framework_bugs.actual: Agents must create HANDOFF.md early to satisfy monitoring_configured, making handoff_written effectively a no-op (the file already exists). The procedural intent of writing the handoff once monitoring is configured is inverted.

no_uncommitted_secrets.details: grep for password|api[_-]?key|secret|token in tracked files only finds template/CI references (e.g. 'NODE_AUTH_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.NPM_TOKEN }}', 'reset their password' in PRODUCT_MANIFESTO.md). No real credentials committed.

log_files_consistent.details: N/A — .claude/process-audit.log absent; no FORCE/reset events occurred (every step completed with proper artifacts)

summary: Base end-to-end run completed Phases 0-4 plus UAT and post-flight; all gates fired correctly when invoked. Single High bug: init.sh non-zero exit due to non-scriptable git_host/visibility prompts after skeleton creation (consistent with the runbook's expected finding). One Medium bug: phase4_release step order requires HANDOFF.md before handoff_written. BL-006 and BL-015 enforcement verified via PreToolUse hook path.
